Job Title: Business Analyst - Fully Remote based in Australia
Company: Good to Great Schools Australia Limited
Ad Text:
Location: Australia, 100% Remote 

About Good to Great Schools Australia (GGSA) 

Good to Great Schools Australia is a dynamic and supportive organisation committed to improving educational outcomes across Australia. We partner with schools and school systems to guide them on their improvement journey from Poor to Fair, Fair to Good, and Good to Great. Our experienced team provides extensive training, coaching, data monitoring, and tools to ensure each partner school develops capable teachers and delivers effective instruction to every child. 

About the Role 

As a Business Analyst at GGSA, you will play a critical role in bridging the gap between business needs and technology solutions. You will be responsible for coordinating with senior stakeholders, designers, and development teams to gather and write detailed requirements, ensuring all project outcomes align with our strategic goals. Additionally, you will take on testing responsibilities and oversee organisational reporting to support data-driven decision-making. 

Key Responsibilities 

Requirement Gathering & Documentation: Collaborate with senior stakeholders to elicit, document, and refine business requirements. Translate these requirements into comprehensive user stories and functional specifications. 

Stakeholder Coordination: Act as a liaison between business units, designers, and technical teams to ensure clear communication and alignment throughout the project lifecycle. 

Testing & Quality Assurance: Develop and execute test plans, manage testing activities, and ensure all deliverables meet the required quality standards. Assist with testing, bug triage, and user acceptance testing (UAT) to ensure quality standards are met by coordinating with development teams and stakeholders to address issues efficiently. 

Organisational Reporting: Produce and manage regular reports on project progress, KPIs, and other relevant metrics, providing insights to senior management and stakeholders. 

Dashboarding & Visualisation: Develop and maintain dashboards using tools like Google Looker and Power BI to provide actionable insights and track key performance indicators. 

Risk Management: Provide support by identifying Identify project risks, develop mitigation strategies, and provide regular status updates to governance bodies. 




About You 

Innovative Problem Solver: Capable of creating solutions that meet diverse stakeholder needs while managing ambiguity and complexity. 

Customer-Centric: Strong focus on understanding and prioritising the customer journey, ensuring all solutions are aligned with customer needs. 

Key Skills and Experience 

Educational Background: Tertiary qualifications in Business, Engineering, or a related field. 

Experience: Minimum of 5 years in a similar role, such as Business Analyst, Product Owner, or Project Manager. 

Methodology Expertise: Strong understanding and practical experience with Agile and Waterfall 

Tools Proficiency: Skilled in using JIRA, Confluence, MIRO, Microsoft Teams, and other collaborative tools. 

Process Improvement: Demonstrated ability to manage processes effectively, focusing on documentation and continuous improvement. 

Communication Skills: Excellent interpersonal skills with the ability to engage stakeholders at all levels. 


Why Join Us? 

At GGSA, you’ll join a team that values transformation and excellence in education. We offer a collaborative environment with opportunities for professional growth, impacting our partners and shaping the future of education in Australia.

ABOUT US

Quantem is Australia's largest bulk liquid storage company with terminals across Australia and New Zealand. We deliver bulk liquid storage solutions for both hazardous and non-hazardous products to a wide array of businesses and primary industries, for both import and export. 

Our Vision is to be a leading, independent bulk commodities storage company in Asia Pacific. 
Our Purpose is to be a trusted and sustainable partner in our customers' supply chain through strategically advantaged infrastructure.
Our Values L.E.A.P.S stands for Leadership, Accountability, Excellence, Partnership & Safety.

More information can be found on the website at www.quantem.com.au

ABOUT THE POSITION

We are looking for a sharp, analytical mind to take on a pivotal role in shaping business decisions through data. As the Financial Reporting Analyst, you won't just report the numbers, you will turn them into meaningful stories that drive action. You will deliver timely, insightful reports that inform both our customers and key internal stakeholders, helping us fine-tune performance and growth.

Your strong accounting knowledge and understanding of financial principles will underpin the accuracy and integrity of reporting, ensuring insights are both commercially meaningful and technically sound.

As the go-to expert for analytics, you will also lead reporting initiatives that directly support business strategy and operational excellence. If you're passionate about transforming data into clear, actionable insights that make a real difference, we want to hear from you. This is a full-time permanent opportunity reporting into the Commercial Finance Manager. 

What you'll be responsible for

Lead the creation of impactful Power BI dashboards and reports from complex data sets
Demonstrating the technical knowledge to create and consolidate key reports to both internal and external stakeholders
Deliver accurate, timely insights to all stakeholders
Ensure data integrity through strong internal controls and governance
Identify and interpret trends to support strategic decision-making
Provide data-driven insights to support sales and tender processes
Constructively challenge assumptions using evidence-based analysis
Maintain and update internal dashboards to reflect real-time performance
Respond proactively to ad hoc reporting needs across the business

About you

Tertiary qualifications in Commerce, Business, Accounting, or a related field
Advanced expertise in Power BI, particularly for automating reporting
Strong background in analytics and operational reporting
Proven ability to deliver accurate, timely client and operational reports
Familiarity with Netsuite and Fusion Finance Systems is a plus
Excellent critical thinking and problem solving skills
Effective time management to meet deadlines
Proactive, self motivated, and resourceful
Strong communication and interpersonal skills for team and client collaboration

Why join Quantem?

Join Quantem for a rewarding career, where our Vision, Purpose, and Values guide us to strive to be an employer of choice in the bulk liquid storage industry. Enjoy some of our exclusive employee benefits, such as:

Play a key role in shaping outcomes across nationally significant infrastructure
Work on complex, high impact projects with strong leadership support
Be part of a safety focused, values driven organisation committed to sustainability and continuous improvement
Hybrid environment (3 to 4 days in our Melbourne CBD Head Office)
Be appreciated in our 'Thanks' Recognition Program
Access to a range of learning and development opportunities
Opportunity to grow in your role and your career
Need transport? Novated lease options available to finance a used or new car
Access to our EAP program for all employees

RECRUITMENT PROCESS

We appreciate attitudes, behaviours, and values that align with Quantem's culture. Preferred applicants will be subject to applicable pre-employment checks. Apply now to align your vision with ours and seize the opportunity to be part of a progressive team!

What's the role?

As the Head of TDD Transformation Enablement, you will play a pivotal role in shaping the future operating model across Technology, Digital & Data at News Corp Australia. This role is responsible for driving enterprise-wide transformation initiatives that improve how teams collaborate, prioritise work, and deliver value faster across the business.

Working across people, process, governance, tooling, and ways of working, you’ll lead large-scale strategic programs that support the evolution toward a more agile, product-led and customer-centric organisation. You’ll partner closely with senior stakeholders across Technology, Product, Editorial and Corporate Functions to drive meaningful change and ensure transformation outcomes align to broader business objectives.

Applications close Tuesday 26 May 2026

Responsibilities

Lead the development, implementation and delivery of the TDD transformation program across the organisation

Drive improvements to operating models, governance frameworks, agile delivery practices and enterprise processes

Provide strategic leadership and trusted advice to senior stakeholders to ensure successful program outcomes

Oversee end-to-end delivery of complex transformation initiatives, ensuring delivery against timelines, budgets and business objectives

Foster collaboration across cross-functional teams to manage dependencies, improve communication, reduce risk and drive continuous improvement

Champion a culture of innovation, adaptability, customer-centric thinking and outcome-focused delivery across Technology, Digital & Data

Who are you?

An experienced transformation leader with 10+ years’ experience delivering large-scale strategic programs within complex, matrixed organisations

A strong people leader with the ability to influence, motivate and guide cross-functional teams through periods of significant change

Commercially minded, with strong business acumen and the ability to align transformation initiatives with broader organisational goals

Highly skilled in stakeholder engagement, communication, change management and storytelling, with the ability to influence at all levels

A strategic and analytical thinker who can identify opportunities for optimisation, establish future-state operating models and drive measurable performance improvements

Experienced in leading enterprise-wide change programs involving agile delivery, governance, process improvement and operational transformation within fast-paced environments

Why News Corp Australia

Join a culture where innovation is our operating rhythm. You’ll work on a diverse portfolio of Australia’s most loved brands and play a pivotal role in leading the technological shift in how we create commercial content. Our vibrant, hybrid work environment and dynamic, values-driven culture empowers you to push boundaries, collaborate as one unified team, and make a positive, measurable impact for all Australians.

News Extras Benefits is a commitment to our people for their valued contribution to News, our audiences, clients and communities.

Our benefits include:

Exclusively ours - Access to publications and products from across the News Corp family

Perks & discounts - Exclusive offers across a range of products and services that deliver genuine cost savings and benefits

Health & wellbeing - Advice, support and facilities for healthy minds and bodies (including an onsite gym at our Adelaide, Brisbane & Sydney offices)

Time off - News offers a range of leave options to support your personal needs and moments that matter in life: Parental leave, Birthday leave, Volunteer leave, Career break and Compassionate leave.

Financial wellbeing - Support on making the most of your money

In addition to our perks and benefits, employees have access to a number of initiatives and programs that support their wellbeing and professional growth, such as:

Helping you grow - Access tailored development opportunities, including leadership programs, to cultivate your skills and support your career

Giving back - Make a difference to our communities through volunteer opportunities and company-organised charity drives

Workplace Inclusion and facilities – Engage with your passions through various employee-resource groups and accessibility to multi-faith quiet rooms & parents’ rooms

Who are we?

News Corp Australia is the nation’s most influential media organisation, operating more than 150 household-name brands across print, digital and multimedia. We lead the way in travel, fashion, food, health, homes and lifestyle, and we are the largest publisher across print and digital, reaching more than 16 million Australians each month.

What’s next?

Apply now or for more information on careers at News Corp Australia visit www.newscorpaustralia.com/careers

Our Commitment to Diversity, Equity and Inclusion

Diversity strengthens our national teams and newsroom as we seek to tell stories that represent the communities of Australia. Our people make us unique, and so we are committed to equal employment opportunities regardless of race, religion, sexual orientation, age, disability or gender identity. Applicants of diverse backgrounds are encouraged to apply!

We are committed to ensuring an accessible and supportive recruitment process. If you require any adjustments or accommodations at any stage, please do not hesitate to let us know here. Company Overview

Centurion is a premier logistics company with a long-standing reputation for excellence in transportation services across Australia. Founded in 1971, our business provides nationwide service with 34 branches across Western Australia, Queensland, Northern Territory, New South Wales,  South Australia and Victoria.

As one of Australia’s largest privately owned transport and logistics companies, we are committed to delivering safe, reliable, and efficient supply chain solutions while fostering a supportive and high-performing workplace culture.

 

About the Role

We are currently looking for an experienced and analytical Senior Contracts and Rates Analyst to join our Finance team based in Welshpool.

In this role, you will play a key part in managing customer rate structures, fuel levy processes, pricing analysis, and contract administration across the business. Working closely with both internal stakeholders and offshore support teams, you will ensure rates are accurately configured, maintained, and applied within Centurion’s systems to support commercial performance and operational efficiency.

This position is ideal for someone who enjoys working with complex data, pricing models, and contract information while collaborating across multiple departments to drive accuracy and continuous improvement.

 

Key Responsibilities

Manage and maintain customer rise and fall processes, including pricing adjustments and calculations
Administer customer and subcontractor fuel levy processes and ensure compliance
Oversee customer rate card configurations and identify pricing discrepancies or revenue leakage
Support offshore Rates Analysts with escalations and system configuration queries
Conduct audits of customer rate cards and pricing structures within the rates system
Perform contract-related pricing calculations and commercial analysis
Coordinate General Rate Increase (GRI) processes for non-contracted customers
Liaise with Business Support and IT teams to resolve system or pricing-related issues
Maintain accurate contract and pricing information across internal systems
Provide reporting, insights, and recommendations to support commercial decision-making
Respond to pricing and contract enquiries from internal stakeholders
Ensure compliance with company policies, contractual obligations, and regulatory requirements

 

Skills and Experience

Minimum 3+ years’ experience in a Rates Analyst, Contracts Analyst, Pricing Analyst, or similar commercial role.
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with high attention to detail
Experience working with pricing models, rate cards, and contract administration
Advanced Microsoft Excel skills, including formulas and data analysis
Strong understanding of commercial and contract management principles
Ability to manage multiple priorities and work to deadlines
Excellent communication and stakeholder engagement skills
Experience within transport, logistics, or related industries is highly regarded
Tertiary qualifications in Commerce, Business, Information Systems, or related discipline preferred

 

Why Join Centurion?

Work for one of Australia’s largest privately owned logistics companies
Supportive team environment with strong leadership
Career development and long-term growth opportunities
Collaborative and fast-paced operational environment
Employee wellness and benefits programs
Safety-focused organisation driven by our RIPPA values

 

How to Apply

If you are ready to take the next step in your career and contribute to a growing national business, click Apply now. For more information, please contact recruitment@centurion.net.au

Don't miss out on this exciting opportunity to join a leader in the transportation industry! We are an Equal Opportunity employer, and we encourage applications from women and First Nations People.

Ad Text:

Great question! We're really proud of the work we do. We are the regulator of the Victorian legal profession. We license lawyers and oversee service standards and conduct. This includes handling complaints about lawyers, investigating poor conduct and overseeing management of trust accounts. We are also the stewards of the public purpose and fidelity funds and administer these to support legal regulation and access to justice in Victoria.

We are a modern best practice regulator, using data and insights to help identify where our actions can have the greatest impact on ensuring ethical and professional legal practice by Victorian lawyers. We are passionate about maintaining and protecting the rule of law by ensuring the integrity of the legal profession.

Okay, and what role are you recruiting for?
We're looking for a Senior Business Analyst to join our Shared Services & Digital Transformation unit. Experience delivering digital transformation initiatives, involving Microsoft Dynamics 365, CRM platforms, or enterprise SaaS solutions
Ability to engage senior stakeholders, facilitate workshops, and align business priorities
Deep understanding of Agile/Scrum/Hybrid delivery frameworks
Strong capability in business process redesign, requirements analysis, data migration planning, and defining integration requirements

That sounds like me! How are you different from other organisations?
We're really excited by our organisation, we offer:

I want to know more!
Sunil Bhandary would love to hear from you on (03) •••• •478 if you have questions about the role.

I'm now really interested!
Our recruitment is all about red carpet not red tape so simply click on ‘Apply Now' before 31/05/2026.

Appointment will be subject to abiding by our Flexible Working Procedure which defines the number of required in-office days at our office (including the ‘team day' of your team) and completion of the VPS Pre-Employment Statutory Declaration, Misconduct Declaration and Consent Form and the Pre-Employment Health Declaration.

VLSB+C is committed to equal employment opportunity, ethical practice, diversity, inclusion and providing reasonable adjustments for persons with disabilities.

ASC is Australia’s submarine company. We built the Royal Australian Navy’s Collins Class submarines, sustain them, and are now delivering their Life of Type Extension to increase the boats’ service lives. We have also been selected by the Australian Government to sustain and jointly build the nation’s conventionally armed, nuclear-powered submarines – the largest and most complex industrial undertaking in Australia’s history. 

Our work is interesting and meaningful, contributing directly to Australia’s national security. We offer rewarding roles that provide the opportunity to grow your career, and the flexibility to balance life and work. A career at ASC is like no other, delivering a deeper purpose we can all be proud of. 

About the Opportunity

We are seeking talented Knowledge Management professionals to join our small, growing team in support of ASC’s pathway to nuclear readiness. As part of a newly established function, this is a unique opportunity to play a hands-on role in shaping how knowledge is captured, shared, and leveraged across a complex and mission-critical environment. This role will contribute to the design, build, and growth of KM capability from the ground up. 

These are full-time opportunities, that can be based at our Osborne site in SA or Henderson in WA 

We are recruiting for two positions: 

1 × Senior Knowledge Management Specialist 
1 × Knowledge Management Specialist 

Both roles will contribute to building a strong knowledge management culture, delivering impactful initiatives, and ensuring critical knowledge is preserved and accessible across the organisation. 

About the roles 

Senior Knowledge Management Specialist 

Reporting to the Knowledge Manager, the Senior Knowledge Management Specialist leads complex, enterprise-wide knowledge management initiatives and helps evolve ASC’s KM capability. 

Key focus areas include: 

Leading cross-functional KM initiatives across the full knowledge lifecycle 
Supporting development and execution of KM strategy aligned to business priorities 
Uplifting KM capability across the organisation 
Driving KM governance, frameworks, and compliance aligned to ISO 30401 and defence requirements 
Influencing senior stakeholders and leading cultural change in knowledge sharing 
This role may include leadership responsibilities and direct reports in the future but currently they don’t 

Knowledge Management Specialist 

Reporting to the Knowledge Manager, the Knowledge Management Specialist supports the delivery of KM initiatives and embedding of KM practices across the business. 

Key focus areas include: 

Supporting and delivering cross-functional KM projects and initiatives 
Capturing and codifying critical knowledge from subject matter experts  
Managing knowledge assets, taxonomy, metadata, and content lifecycle  
Facilitating communities of practice, lessons learned, and knowledge sharing activities  
Providing training, support and guidance to uplift KM capability  

About You 

For both roles 

Tertiary qualifications in Knowledge Management, Information Systems, Engineering, Project/Change Management, or a similar discipline or equivalent experience 
Strong experience in knowledge capture, transfer, and content management practices  
Experience working in complex project, engineering or heavy industry environments (ideally from a defence environment) 
Ability to translate complex or technical information into accessible knowledge assets  
Experience with digital collaboration tools (e.g., SharePoint, Confluence, PLM platforms) 
You are a positive, motivated individual 

Additional for Senior KM role 

7+ years’ experience in knowledge management 
Proven ability to lead enterprise-level KM initiatives and influence senior stakeholders  
Experience developing KM frameworks, governance, and maturity uplift approaches  
Demonstrated leadership, coaching, and mentoring capability 

Additional for KM role 

5+ years’ experience in a relevant technical or project environment  
Strong capability in facilitating KM processes such as lessons learned and communities of practice  

Eligibility 

This role requires a current National Police Clearance and an Australian Defence Security Clearance to a NV1 level. Your eligibility for this role is dependent on you obtaining and maintaining such clearances. 

To check your eligibility please visit the Australian Government Security Vetting Agency website. 

Benefits 

Unique and challenging work with a deeper purpose. 
Flexibility over how, when and where you work for the best work/life balance. 
Up to 10 days additional leave every year so you can rest and recharge. 
Generous parental leave so you can give your family the focus it deserves. 
Subsidised childcare to ease the financial pressure on your family. 

Apply today for a career with a deeper purpose. 

Early applications are encouraged as shortlisting will commence immediately.

Through the merger of Bank Australia and Qudos Bank we’re creating a better, stronger and more resilient bank for our customers and employees. As one company with two trusted brands, we’re building on the strong foundations of our history and shared values as we bring together the best of both banks.

The IRRBB & Liquid Assets Analyst supports the Head of Balance Sheet & Capital in managing the Bank’s interest rate risk in the banking book (IRRBB), structural hedging activities and long‑term liquid asset portfolio. The role provides analysis, monitoring, recommendations and execution across IRRBB exposures, hedge performance, liquid asset investments and overall balance sheet positioning. It contributes to the development and maintenance of the Bank’s IRRBB framework and liquid asset strategy, including analysis of repricing, basis and yield curve risks, hedge effectiveness and investment performance to support informed risk management and decision‑making.

Why join us

We have big plans to become Australia’s most trusted bank and a leading purpose-driven business.
As a certified B Corp, we’re part of a global business community who meet the highest social and environmental standards, putting purpose over profit.
Every role and person in our bank is essential to bringing our values, purpose and aspiration to life.
We offer flexible working options, competitive salary and 14% super.
Our staff have access to a range of meaningful benefits to support their physical and mental wellbeing including 24/7 free counselling, free flu vaccinations and discounted gym memberships.
We also support staff with study assistance, paid parental leave (regardless of gender), volunteer, bereavement, gender affirmation and family violence leave; and the opportunity for bonus annual leave.
We support our staff to grow their careers through learning and development and an organisational culture where we reward and recognise innovation and values, not long hours.
We are committed to building an inclusive culture and a diverse workforce that reflects the Australian community.
We care for a conservation reserve – where we work with key partner organisations to protect threatened species and involve staff in conservation.

What you will be doing

Analyse and monitor IRRBB exposures (repricing, basis and yield curve risk), supporting framework maintenance, stress testing and risk reporting (e.g. NIIS, EVE).
Analyse the Bank’s IRRBB profile and prepare derivative hedging actions.  Subject to individual dealing mandate negotiate and executed derivative hedging transactions with wholesale counterparties.
Support development and implementation of structural hedging strategies, including analysis of swaps, derivatives and hedge effectiveness.
Manage and provide insights on the long‑term liquid asset portfolio, assessing composition, duration, yield, liquidity and investment opportunities.
Monitor and manage counterparty credit risk exposures, including limits, utilisation, ratings and emerging risks.
Support balance sheet forecasting and scenario analysis, providing insights on pricing, funding, growth and market impacts on IRRBB and liquidity outcomes.
Prepare high‑quality reporting, analysis and recommendations for senior stakeholders, including ALCO and the Group Treasurer.
Maintain governance frameworks, policies and limits relating to IRRBB, hedging and liquid assets, ensuring alignment with regulatory expectations.
Collaborate across Treasury, Finance, Risk and external stakeholders to support effective risk management, decision‑making and regulatory engagement.

What you will bring

Strong analytical mindset with the ability to interpret complex interest rate risk, liquid asset portfolios and market dynamics.
High attention to detail and ownership of outputs, with the ability to identify, assess and escalate emerging risks.
Ability to translate technical Treasury concepts into clear, practical recommendations for stakeholders.
Collaborative approach, working effectively across Treasury, Finance, Risk and Technology teams.
Solid understanding of IRRBB, structural hedging, and long‑term liquid asset portfolio management (e.g. bonds, FRNs, duration, yield curve risk).
Strong financial modelling capability, including advanced Excel and experience with Treasury/ALM systems (e.g. QRM or equivalent).
Knowledge of prudential standards (e.g. APS 117, APS 210) and experience operating within regulated ADI environments.
Tertiary qualification in finance, economics, commerce or related discipline, with experience in Treasury, financial markets or balance sheet management highly regarded.
Commitment to Bank Australia’s purpose, aspiration, values and brand as well as the B Corp ethos.

By submitting an application you agree to Bank Australia’s Privacy Policy for applicants and confirm that you are legally able to work in Australia. Bank Australia is an equal opportunity employer committed to sustainable development. We encourage people from different backgrounds to apply, including Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people, people from different cultural backgrounds and people with disabilities because we want to reflect the diversity of our communities. We are committed to making reasonable adjustments to provide a positive, barrier-free recruitment process and supportive workplace.

Bank Australia reserves the right to withdraw this advertisement at any time. Candidates will be progressed as applications are received, rather than after the advertisement closure date. If you wish to be considered, please submit your application as soon as possible. Please note, the successful candidate will be subject to satisfactory background checks including but not limited to police and previous employment.

Job Title: Lead Consultant – D365 F & SCM Finance
Ad Text:
About the Company

We are HCLTech, one of the fastest-growing large tech companies in the world and home to 219,000+ people across 54 countries, supercharging progress through industry-leading capabilities centered around Digital, Engineering and Cloud.

Job Summary

The Lead Consultant in D365 F&SCM Finance plays a crucial role in developing and delivering tailored financial solutions that meet client requirements. This position requires advanced proficiency in D365 Finance and Supply Chain Management, enabling the consultant to address complex client challenges while ensuring high-quality deliverables within stipulated timelines. Ad Text:
Our Client

Yumbah is proudly positioned as Australia’s leading aquaculture company. With operations across South Australia, Tasmania, Victoria and New South Wales, they sustainably farm abalone, oysters, mussels and kingfish for both domestic and international markets, and supply oyster spat to the South Australian oyster industry. Driven by their core values of integrity, daring and teamwork, we are committed to supporting our people and delivering a sustainable source of premium seafood protein to our customers.

An opportunity now exists for an experienced Senior Financial Analyst – FP&A to join the team based in Kent Town on a full time, permanent basis.

The Role

Yumbah is a growth-stage aquaculture business in a period of rapid expansion, supported by both acquisitions and ongoing organic growth. This trajectory is underpinned by a finance function that is continuing to be enhanced.

This role acts as the dedicated FP&A lead for a species division, with exposure across other areas over time. Partnering closely with the FP&A Manager, Species GM, and COO, you will own divisional planning, fair value assessment, integrated budgeting and forecasting, and monthly performance reporting to the executive and Board. You’ll be responsible for:

Translate operational and biological performance into clear financial outcomes for the assigned species division
Own the monthly Mass Balance refresh, S&OP cycle, and fair value assessment
Build and maintain the integrated three-way financial model for the division
Partner with the Species GM, COO, and operational leaders on budget, forecast, capex, and pricing decisions
Provide constructive challenge on variance drivers, forecasts, and operational assumptions
Contribute to the ongoing uplift of the FP&A function alongside the CFO and FP&A Manager
Act as the analytical backbone for a highly complex, operationally driven aquaculture business during a significant growth phase
Play a key role in shaping and strengthening a finance function in active build
About You

5 + years of experience as a Senior Analyst with strong financial modelling, business partnering and commercial advisory experience
Sector experience is not essential to be considered. Yumbah will invest in teaching you this providing your modelling and analytical foundations are strong.
Candidates from FMCG manufacturing, agribusiness, mining and resources, energy, infrastructure, healthcare, financial services, professional services and big 4 are all encouraged to apply
Architect's instinct. You think in systems and dependencies before detail. You design for replication rather than solving in isolation.
Constructive challenger. You are willing to push back on operational assumptions, capex business cases and forecast inputs with evidence-based analysis, while keeping working relationships intact.
Operational fluency. You will be on pens, in hatcheries, walking oyster racks. Good FP&A here is impossible without operational context, and that means time on site, not just time in spreadsheets.
Tech curiosity. You treat AI-assisted modelling, BI tools and workflow automation as professional leverage, not buzzwords or threats. You will help evolve our toolkit, not just use it.
Builder mindset. You are energised by ambiguity and active build, not destabilised by it. Templates and methodology will change during your tenure. You will help shape that change.
In Return

Strong opportunity to develop your expertise in integrated, three-way modelling across a complex, national business and AASB141 fair value methodology
AI and tech forward business with a focus on continuous improvement
Direct exposure to acquisition integration during the largest growth period in the company’s history
CFO adjacent visibility on a key species, with line of sight into Board reporting, audit interaction and strategic decision making
Adelaide based role in Kent Town with travel to regional and operational sites as required
Remuneration is competitive and aligned to a senior FP&A role of this complexity
Next Steps
 
If you’re interested in the role on offer, please apply using the relevant link. If you have any questions relating to the role, please email Megan Kivell – mki••••@ppsconsulting.com.au

AEMO at the Heart of Energy 

AEMO is Australia’s independent energy system and market operator and system planner. AEMO’s purpose is to ensure safe, reliable and affordable energy today, and enable the energy transition for the benefit of all Australians.  Australian governments are now committed to a net zero economy for Australia by 2050 and our vision is to enable this. We have the once-in-a-lifetime opportunity to co-design the future of our energy systems, and our core values revolve around character, commitment, and connection. Join us as we contribute to this significant transition in the energy sector.    

About the role  

The WA CIA team is responsible to operate the Reserve Capacity Mechanism (RCM), a unique feature of the Wholesale Electricity Market (WEM) and pivotal to its operations. The RCM ensures sufficient energy producing systems and demand side management capacity is available to support reliable electricity supply in the WA grid.  

In this dynamic role, you will get to work with a smart, diverse and supportive group of people while actively leading and contributing to key processes that have a significant financial outcome on the WEM and its stakeholders. You will be actively supporting important reform initiatives aimed at transitioning WA’s electricity sector to a zero-carbon future.  

Be prepared for a cerebral workout most days as you use your expertise and regulatory understanding of the WEM to drive outcomes that support the secure and reliable supply of electricity in the WEM. 

What you will be doing 

Drive the development and present strategic comprehensive analysis to support a wide range of RCM functions and inform related decision-making processes.

Provide expert advice to internal and external stakeholders in formal/informal forums, providing insights into the technical, operational, and commercial aspects of the RCM, including risk assessments, drafting relevant material (memos, briefing notes, presentations, etc.) and recommendations to the management and senior leadership team in a timely manner.

Lead/undertake thorough reviews of technical documents, adhering to strict due-diligence, compliance with the regulatory instruments and confidentiality standards.

Identify, recommend and implement process improvements that minimise compliance risks, enhance operational efficiency, and strengthen organisational capabilities.

Lead the development of and maintain effective working relationships with external stakeholders that include retailers, generators, the network operator, and the policy maker, and support AEMO’s involvement in government policy initiatives and WEM regulatory matters. 

What you will bring to the role   

Understanding of the political and regulatory framework in which the energy industry operates, the operation of energy markets and power systems, and a comprehensive understanding of the changes in the energy sector and how these affect AEMO.

Demonstrated experience in delivering compliance while undertaking analysis, following procedures and developing reports in a commercial/regulatory environment.

Demonstrated ability to develop high quality written material and deliver effective and clear presentations which communicate highly technical, complicated concepts or issues to varied audiences.

Demonstrated experience successfully leading complicated technical and/or operational activities/projects by working effectively in large teams and across organisations.

Passion for energy with a strong attention to detail, a resilient and collegiate team member that actively coaches others in the team while being self-motivated and focussed.  

In return some of our benefits to you  

Flexible working: work from home, part time, job share, hybrid options, and additional leave options 

Professional development via projects, industry networks, job rotation, study assistance and more. 

Give back with up to 4 days of volunteering leave per year.  

Reward your hard work with annual performance bonuses (subject to eligibility). 

Embrace a healthier you with our wellness program, discounted health insurance, gym perks and our comprehensive Employee Assistance Program (EAP).  

About Our Process   

At AEMO we are committed to creating a diverse and inclusive workforce. We welcome people of all backgrounds, including people with disabilities and neurodivergence and those who identify as Aboriginal and/or Torres Strait Islander. If you have any accessibility requirements to support you during the application or interview process, simply let us know by emailing us at tal•••@aemo.com.au

Ad Text:
Shape Strategy. Drive Growth. Deliver Impact.

ADG is seeking a commercially minded Group Strategy Analyst to join our Strategy & Corporate Development team. This is a dynamic, high-impact role focused on group strategy, valuation, acquisitions, funding, and key strategic initiatives.

You will be delivering high-quality strategic and valuation outputs that are trusted by the Executive team and Board. You’ll ensure group strategy and valuation reviews are completed on time with clear, data-driven insights. Your work will maintain a strong and active acquisition pipeline, backed by robust financial analysis. You will play a key role in monitoring ADG’s funding position, proactively identifying risks or refinancing needs, and supporting strategic decisions that drive long-term value for the organisation.

What You’ll Do

Strategy & Planning: Lead the annual and mid-year group strategy reviews and support business unit planning.
Valuation: Own and manage the ADG valuation model, including updates, forecasts, and stakeholder engagement.
Acquisitions: Maintain ADG’s acquisition pipeline, conduct financial assessments, coordinate due diligence, and support bid modelling.
Strategic Initiatives: Provide market research and financial analysis for new opportunities and emerging sectors.
Funding: Monitor funding metrics, support refinancing, and assess funding impacts of investments.
Collaboration & Risk: Work closely with executives, advisors, and internal teams, while applying ADG’s risk, safety, and cyber security frameworks.

What You’ll Bring

7+ years in investment banking, consulting, or corporate finance.
Bachelor’s degree in Finance, Economics, Engineering, or related field.
Post-grad/industry qualifications (CFA, MBA, CA) preferred.
Strong skills in financial modelling, valuation, and strategic planning.
Excellent communication, stakeholder engagement, and attention to detail.
Experience with energy sector feasibility studies (desirable).

Why ADG?

At Airport Development Group, we take pride in creating a workplace that supports growth, balance, and wellbeing. You’ll be part of a values-driven organisation that fosters collaboration and continuous learning. From ongoing development opportunities and a supportive leadership team to additional leave entitlements for eligible staff, we believe in recognising and rewarding your contributions. We also offer access to a comprehensive wellbeing program and salary packaging options.

Working at ADG means being part of a team that connects communities across the Northern Territory and your work will directly contribute to our commitment to safety, compliance, and operational excellence.

Ready to Apply?

If this sounds like the opportunity for you, we’d love to hear from you. Submit your application with your CV and a brief cover letter outlining your suitability for the role.

Join Us & Make an Impact!

At ADG we embrace equality, diversity and inclusion. We encourage applications from Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples, people with disabilities, and individuals from culturally diverse backgrounds.

Join a forward-thinking team where your expertise will make a real impact. If you're a self-motivated professional with a passion for Aviation Safety and Standards, we’d love to hear from you.

Agencies should note that unsolicited resumes or floated profiles will not be accepted for this position and ADG will not pay any fees related to resumes that are submitted directly to hiring managers and not through our approved process. Where appropriate, the Talent Manager will work with our preferred panel of agencies to source candidates for roles.

ASC is Australia’s submarine company. We built the Royal Australian Navy’s Collins Class submarines, sustain them, and are now delivering their Life of Type Extension to increase the boats’ service lives. We have also been selected by the Australian Government to sustain and jointly build the nation’s conventionally armed, nuclear-powered submarines – the largest and most complex industrial undertaking in Australia’s history. 

Our work is interesting and meaningful, contributing directly to Australia’s national security. We offer rewarding roles that provide the opportunity to grow your career, and the flexibility to balance life and work. A career at ASC is like no other, delivering a deeper purpose we can all be proud of. 

About the Opportunity

We are seeking talented Knowledge Management professionals to join our small, growing team in support of ASC’s pathway to nuclear readiness. As part of a newly established function, this is a unique opportunity to play a hands-on role in shaping how knowledge is captured, shared, and leveraged across a complex and mission-critical environment. This role will contribute to the design, build, and growth of KM capability from the ground up. 

These are full-time opportunities, that can be based at our Osborne site in SA or Henderson in WA 

We are recruiting for two positions: 

1 × Senior Knowledge Management Specialist 
1 × Knowledge Management Specialist 

Both roles will contribute to building a strong knowledge management culture, delivering impactful initiatives, and ensuring critical knowledge is preserved and accessible across the organisation. 

About the roles 

Senior Knowledge Management Specialist 

Reporting to the Knowledge Manager, the Senior Knowledge Management Specialist leads complex, enterprise-wide knowledge management initiatives and helps evolve ASC’s KM capability. 

Key focus areas include: 

Leading cross-functional KM initiatives across the full knowledge lifecycle 
Supporting development and execution of KM strategy aligned to business priorities 
Uplifting KM capability across the organisation 
Driving KM governance, frameworks, and compliance aligned to ISO 30401 and defence requirements 
Influencing senior stakeholders and leading cultural change in knowledge sharing 
This role may include leadership responsibilities and direct reports in the future but currently they don’t 

Knowledge Management Specialist 

Reporting to the Knowledge Manager, the Knowledge Management Specialist supports the delivery of KM initiatives and embedding of KM practices across the business. 

Key focus areas include: 

Supporting and delivering cross-functional KM projects and initiatives 
Capturing and codifying critical knowledge from subject matter experts  
Managing knowledge assets, taxonomy, metadata, and content lifecycle  
Facilitating communities of practice, lessons learned, and knowledge sharing activities  
Providing training, support and guidance to uplift KM capability  

About You 

For both roles 

Tertiary qualifications in Knowledge Management, Information Systems, Engineering, Project/Change Management, or a similar discipline or equivalent experience 
Strong experience in knowledge capture, transfer, and content management practices  
Experience working in complex project, engineering or heavy industry environments (ideally from a defence environment) 
Ability to translate complex or technical information into accessible knowledge assets  
Experience with digital collaboration tools (e.g., SharePoint, Confluence, PLM platforms) 
You are a positive, motivated individual 

Additional for Senior KM role 

7+ years’ experience in knowledge management 
Proven ability to lead enterprise-level KM initiatives and influence senior stakeholders  
Experience developing KM frameworks, governance, and maturity uplift approaches  
Demonstrated leadership, coaching, and mentoring capability 

Additional for KM role 

5+ years’ experience in a relevant technical or project environment  
Strong capability in facilitating KM processes such as lessons learned and communities of practice  

Eligibility 

This role requires a current National Police Clearance and an Australian Defence Security Clearance to a NV1 level. Your eligibility for this role is dependent on you obtaining and maintaining such clearances. 

To check your eligibility please visit the Australian Government Security Vetting Agency website. 

Benefits 

Unique and challenging work with a deeper purpose. 
Flexibility over how, when and where you work for the best work/life balance. 
Up to 10 days additional leave every year so you can rest and recharge. 
Generous parental leave so you can give your family the focus it deserves. 
Subsidised childcare to ease the financial pressure on your family. 

Apply today for a career with a deeper purpose. 

Early applications are encouraged as shortlisting will commence immediately.

Drive impactful pricing decisions with expert analysis, modelling, and insight into natural peril risks.

 

The role

The Natural Perils Pricing Actuary / Specialist will support the Pricing team and company on insurance pricing decisions through robust, accurate and timely data driven analysis and insight. This senior technical role supports the pricing, underwriting and risk management of natural peril risks through data analysis, modelling, technical review, research of emerging data and methods, and providing appropriate advice to senior management.


Location: Sydney or Brisbane 

What experience you’ll bring

General

To undertake analytical tasks efficiently, thoroughly, with due care and minimal mistakes. This includes: 

Develop industry-leading pricing solutions for natural peril risks
Provide pivotal actuarial advice and support, with a focus on technical excellence, integrity and continuous improvement
Analyse, interpret, and extrapolate data from a number of sources to resolve complex issues
Manage data, conduct complex technical analysis, draw insights and apply commercial acumen
Implement rating changes to the live pricing system, including testing and post-live monitoring, and quantify the expected impact on the portfolio.
Perform regular and ad-hoc reporting
Prepare and present papers to communicate results, insights, and recommendations to technical and non-technical audiences.
Support wider business with technical queries related to pricing and natural peril risk.
Engage key stakeholders, communicate findings and recommend next steps
Collaborate with analytical colleagues across the company (e.g. underwriting, sales, marketing, IT)

SAS/EDW/GCP/Data Lake

Ensure data is delivered in a timely and robust manner
Consider and evaluate improvements to pricing infrastructure as it relates to external data (e.g. new functionality, alternate software platforms)
Engage with business to enhance the value and utilisation of data for pricing and other use cases 

Other

Perform other reasonable and relevant activities as requested from time to time to meet operational requirements.
Adhere to all pricing policies and procedures
Test IT changes and effectively identify potential issues before go-live
Mentor and guide junior team members.

 

What You’ll Bring to the Team

Essential

Minimum 5 years’ experience in general insurance pricing
Demonstrable experience in natural perils risk pricing and critical evaluation of associated data sources
Degree qualified in mathematics, actuarial studies, data science or similar
Programming knowledge (e.g. SAS, R, Python, GCP) for sophisticated model development
Proven analytical and problem solving skills 

Desirable

Excellent written and verbal communication skills, particularly for translating technical insights to diverse audience
Excellent attention to detail with emphasis on work traceability and governance

 

Our Perks 

The benefits of working at A&G 

Be rewarded - we recognise high performance and reward our people for their hard work through bonuses and other perks.
Access to A&G’s employee reward and recognition platform, powered by Rewards Gateway - Enjoy a wide range of benefits, including: 

Personalised rewards and peer-to-peer recognition
Exclusive discounts and savings across more than 600 top retailers
A comprehensive recognition program that celebrates milestones and achievements—helping you feel valued every step of the way
Work flexibility - with options to work from home up to two days per week.
Options for leave - life happens, so we’ve got volunteer days, an additional paid ‘ME’ day, paid parental leave and the opportunity to purchase additional leave to cover all of the big stuff.
Grow with us - we’ve got learning and professional development opportunities to suit everyone.
Give back - our A&G Difference program gives you the power to change our community for the better through volunteering, fundraising and donation opportunities for causes that you’re passionate about.
Take care of yourself - your wellbeing is important to us and our healthy mind and body hub, mental-health support and fitness discounts will help you be your best self.
Celebrate the wins - we love sharing our successes and celebrating together - join us and you’ve got a ticket to our many on-site events throughout the year, family fun days and annual celebrations.
Save money - as well as discounts on insurance products, we’ve teamed up with some incredible retailers, hospitality providers and others to bring you discounts on your purchases, no matter where you are in Australia.
 

About us  
Auto & General (A&G) is the fastest-growing major Motor and Home insurer in Australia, providing insurance products and solutions to safeguard a brighter future for our customers and community. 

Our range of general insurance products including Car, Motorcycle, Home, Contents Pet and Travel products are delivered through our multi-award-winning brand Budget Direct and partnerships with leading brands - ING, Qantas and Coles Insurance.  

We’re excited about the future and we’re always on the lookout for talented, passionate individuals who can help us achieve our goal of being Australia’s best insurer! If this sounds like you, apply today.  
Auto & General values individual differences and believes in fostering an inclusive culture that creates a great place to work for all.

Senior Analyst / Manager (full time) – Data Science (multiple positions)

📍 Australia (Remote/hybrid) – SA, ACT, NSW, VIC, QLD, TAS, WA and NT

About the role

We are seeking to appoint multiple Senior Analyst and Manager roles to join our growing data analytics practice. The roles offer the opportunity to work closely with senior stakeholders across industry and government, delivering high-impact analytics solutions that support policy and commercial decision-making.

You will play a key role across both client delivery and business growth, with the opportunity to shape how we build and scale our capabilities.

About us

We are a start-up specialist data analytics and modelling consultancy, helping organisations make better decisions through robust, transparent, and actionable insights. Our work spans predictive analytics, financial modelling, and data-driven decision support across both industry and government.

We combine deep technical expertise with strong commercial understanding, enabling us to tackle complex problems and deliver solutions that are not only technically sound, but also practical and decision-ready. Our approach is highly collaborative—we work closely with clients, often embedding within their teams, to ensure outcomes are aligned, sustainable, and fit for purpose.

Learn more at modax.com.au.

Key responsibilities

Deliver high-quality analytics solutions across a range of projects

Apply machine learning, predictive modelling, and quantitative techniques to solve complex public policy or business problems

Contribute to the preparation of business proposals, including background research, structuring approaches, and drafting content tailored to client needs

Support the continuous improvement of internal delivery processes, templates, and methodologies to enhance efficiency, quality, and consistency

Project and team leadership (Manager level)

Manage end-to-end project delivery to ensure projects are delivered on time and on budget

Coach and mentor junior team members, providing technical guidance and oversight

Support quality assurance and review of deliverables

About you

Essential requirements

A strong quantitative background (e.g. actuarial science, data science, mathematics, statistics, physics, computer science or similar)

Senior Analyst: 3+ years' experience

Manager: 5+ years' experience

Australian citizenship to obtain security clearance

Proficiency in Python, SQL and/or SAS (experience with additional programming languages is highly regarded)

Strong capability in machine learning and predictive modelling

Strong work ethics, proactive and a team player

Willing to work on-site at client locations as required

Desirable experience

Consulting or client-facing experience

Experience working with large, complex datasets and building production-ready predictive models

Financial modelling experience or strong Excel skills

Why join us

Competitive remuneration (salary + super + bonus)

Be part of a values-driven, flat-structured team where your opinions are valued

Work directly with senior stakeholders on high-impact, real-world problems

Opportunity to take ownership and shape both client outcomes and internal capability

Flexible, remote working arrangements across Australia

A collaborative, high-trust environment with strong focus on quality and professional growth

How to apply

If you're interested, we'd love to hear from you. Please submit your CV and a short note outlining your experience and interest in the role.

Head of Quantitative
B2B Financial Services Technology | Global Scale | Sydney or Melbourne

Greenfield opportunity to define a global quantitative capability


Direct influence on product strategy and international expansion


Work with sophisticated institutional clients at scale


A high-growth B2B financial services technology company is appointing a Head of Quantitative in a newly created role to build and lead its next-generation modelling capability.

Operating at the intersection of wealth management, actuarial science and enterprise software, this business partners with tier-one financial institutions across Australia, the UK and Europe to modernise how financial advice is delivered at scale.

This is a rare opportunity to architect stochastic modelling frameworks from first principles, while building and leading a world-class quantitative function with global reach.

The Opportunity
The business is at a pivotal inflection point and is poised for continued market traction.

Having successfully deployed deterministic advice models at institutional scale, the next phase of growth requires the introduction of sophisticated stochastic modelling to unlock new markets, new products and materially improved customer outcomes.

As Head of Quantitative, you will:

Define the quantitative strategy


Lead the technical uplift to stochastic modelling


Build and scale a high-performing team


Act as the ultimate authority on model risk, integrity and regulatory alignment
This is a genuine player–coach role, with significant hands-on technical ownership.

What You’ll Be Responsible For
Architect and deliver stochastic modelling frameworks (Monte Carlo, scenario generation, multi-goal optimisation)


Lead the transition from deterministic Excel-based models to scalable calculation engines


Translate complex regulatory and advice policy requirements into robust quantitative logic


Build, mentor and scale a quantitative team across Australia and global markets


Partner closely with Product and Engineering to productionise models at scale


Engage directly with tier-one institutional clients, actuarial teams and regulators


Own quantitative governance, validation and model risk standards globally
What It Will Take to Make an Impact
Deep expertise in stochastic / actuarial / quantitative modelling


Strong hands-on background in tools such as Excel, Python or actuarial modelling platforms


Experience operating in regulated financial services environments (wealth, retirement, insurance or advice technology)


Proven ability to lead technical teams while remaining hands-on


Confidence engaging with C-level stakeholders, institutional clients and regulators


Comfort operating in ambiguity within a high-growth, product-led business


Advanced qualifications in Actuarial Science, Mathematics, Statistics or a related quantitative field are highly regarded.

If you’re motivated by complex problems, real-world impact and global scale, this is a rare leadership opportunity.  Call Michelle Rubinstein at The Onset on 04•• ••• 043 for a confidential discussion or apply online.

About the role



AGL has an exciting opportunity for an experienced Business Improvement Specialist to join our Risk & Assurance - Business Transformation team, helping drive operational excellence and continuous improvement at Bayswater Power Station.



This role is based on site at Bayswater Power Station (Muswellbrook), working closely with frontline operations and cross‑functional stakeholders to identify, design and implement practical improvements that optimise performance, reduce cost and strengthen ways of working.



You'll be part of a collaborative, high‑impact team focused on improving overall business performance while ensuring maximum efficiency and effectiveness - aligned to AGL's values of Bring on tomorrow, Can Do. Will Do., Be Safe. Be Supportive.




What you will be doing

As a Business Improvement Specialist, you will partner with site leaders and cross‑functional teams to analyse current processes, identify improvement opportunities, and implement initiatives that deliver measurable outcomes across operations.

Key aspects of the role include:


Analysing business processes to identify inefficiencies, risks, and opportunities for improvement

Developing and implementing strategies that enhance operations and efficiency, supporting optimisation and cost reduction

Collaborating with cross‑functional teams (e.g., operations, maintenance, IT and other departments) to deliver practical process improvements

Monitoring and evaluating effectiveness of implemented improvements, tracking impact and embedding continuous improvement

Developing and maintaining documentation for business processes and standards to drive consistency and adoption

Using data analytics and visualisation tools (Power BI, Tableau or Excel) to support insights, prioritisation and decision‑making

Preparing reports and communicating findings and recommendations to senior management and key stakeholders





What you will bring

To succeed in this role, you'll combine strong analytical capability with practical improvement delivery experience and the ability to influence and drive change across diverse stakeholder groups.


Tertiary qualification in Business, Management, Engineering, Operations or a related field

3+ years' experience delivering process improvement outcomes in previous roles (Change to 3+ years)

Certification in Change Management and/or process improvement methodologies such as Six Sigma, Lean, or Design Thinking (highly regarded)

Strong analytical and critical thinking skills, with a structured approach to problem solving

Highly developed communication and presentation skills, with confidence engaging stakeholders from the frontline through to senior leaders

Project management and team leadership / influencing capability, with a collaborative approach to delivering outcomes through others

Experience in data analysis and visualisation tools such as Power BI, Tableau or Excel

Sound knowledge of SAP and the ability to leverage system data to support decisions and improvement initiatives

Familiarity with process improvement methodologies and tools, and a continuous improvement mindset
